channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: WWDC 2026: Apple makes its big Siri AI reveal, changes Liquid Glass and more
item.description: Follow Apple WWDC 2026 live updates as the keynote spotlights AI, software, Siri and developer tools and likely CEO John Ternus's first major appearance.

item.title: Netanyahu says war with Iran, Hezbollah isn't over after Tehran says it's halting strikes
item.description: Iran and Israel traded strikes Sunday night for the first time since the U.S. and Iran agreed to a fragile ceasefire in mid-April.

item.title: Convicted FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried files formal request for Trump pardon
item.description: The co-founder of the now defunct FTX crypto exchange is currently serving a 25-year federal prison sentence.

item.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq gain as chipmakers rebound from rout, Iran halts Israel attacks: Live updates
item.description: Investors will also be focused on inflation data and the public debut of SpaceX on Friday.

item.title: Four high-yield savings accounts cut their rates. Where you can still earn 4%
item.description: BTIG said it was surprised at the recent rate reductions from high-yield savings accounts. Here are the latest annual percentage yields.

item.title: Judge blocks Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee
item.description: Trump had imposed the $100,000 fee in order to restrict the program, arguing that it has undermined U.S. security through the "replacement of American workers."

item.title: Tom Lee's clients are worried SpaceX IPO will mark a market top. He's not
item.description: The Fundstrat managing partner and head of research said that there's enough cash on the sidelines that public markets can absorb the Elon Musk company's debut.

item.title: Silicon Valley’s new buyout playbook is hitting Wall Street
item.description: Instead of selling AI tools to companies, venture firms are buying legacy companies outright and rebuilding them around AI from the inside.

item.title: Chip rebound sparks hedging flurry from traders
item.description: Despite the overall bearish sentiment on the cohort, traders put a few bullish bets on potential semiconductor winners.

item.title: Food supply 'not at risk' after new Texas screwworm cases, USDA secretary says
item.description: The U.S. will lean on the same playbook it did starting in the late 1950s, part of which involves releasing sterile insects to suppress the pest's population.

item.title: Retirement 'underspending' is risky, advisor says. Here's why
item.description: Retirees often focus on the risk of spending too much in retirement. But spending too little poses another kind of danger.

item.title: China is helping to cushion global oil prices below $100 — but analysts warn it won’t last
item.description: China has reduced oil imports since the start of the Iran war, capping global crude prices.

item.title: Strong earthquake strikes off Cuba's northwest coast
item.description: The U.S. Geological Survey reported the earthquake at a 6.1 magnitude, with its epicenter 104 km west-northwest of Mantua, Cuba.

item.title: Airlines find the grass isn't always greener with new engines
item.description: Airline CEOs complained that manufacturers aren't making enough of their engines and that they're falling short on reliability.

item.title: SpaceX top? Major IPOs don't typically flag a bull market peak, history shows
item.description: Historical data suggest that major IPOs don't usually flag a bull market peak in the year that follows.

item.title: Chip rebound has one trader buying protection
item.description: Chip stocks are bounding back Monday, but the rebound doesn't mean tech investors are in the clear, according to Mike Khouw.

item.title: Household worries over finances hit highest level since July 2022, New York Fed survey shows
item.description: While the central bank's monthly survey showed the inflation outlook mostly unchanged, the general perception of conditions deteriorated.

item.title: Weight loss drug maker sinks 23% after new safety data spooks investors
item.description: While the experimental drug met its key targets, analysts are concerned over the commercial opportunity due to side effects.

item.title: Kalshi traders say Strait of Hormuz traffic won't return to normal before January
item.description: Two weeks ago traders on Kalshi saw a 60% chance that the Strait of Hormuz would open before August. Now traders see a higher chance it won't open before 2027.

item.title: Congress moves toward approving $70 billion for ICE and CBP through Trump's presidency
item.description: The U.S. House is expected to vote this week on a $70 billion measure to fund Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Customs and Border Protection after delay.

item.title: We're getting closer to upgrading Honeywell as shares pull back into its breakup
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Is a reverse mortgage right for me?
item.description: Reverse mortgages can help people tap their home equity without a monthly payment, but they're risky.

item.title: Veteran trader Jay Woods has a warning for investors looking to play the SpaceX IPO
item.description: The chief market strategist at Freedom Capital Markets is looking at the rocket company's debut with caution.

item.title: Oil prices ease after Iran says military operations against Israel are over
item.description: Oil rises on Monday amid heightened tensions in the Middle East, raising concerns that the conflict may drag on.

item.title: Novo and Lilly are competing to win the GLP-1 pill market as they prepare for Medicare coverage
item.description: Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly bring their longtime rivalry to GLP-1 pills and prepare to make their pitch to seniors with Medicare.

item.title: Corning shares jump 4% after company strikes deal to power Amazon AI data centers in U.S.
item.description: Amazon is the latest megacap company to announce a massive deal with Corning, which is rapidly becoming a critical player in the AI buildout.

item.title: USAA to return nearly $1 billion to Florida members as legal reforms help lower insurance costs
item.description: USAA said it will deliver nearly $1 billion in combined savings and returns to eligible Florida members, including a $500 million dividend, CNBC can exclusively report.

item.title: United CEO brushes off airline mergers after American rejection: 'There's nothing'
item.description: When asked about a wave of consolidation among airlines, Kirby said further combination opportunities look unlikely: "There's nothing."

item.title: This startup wants to reduce payment friction on prediction markets
item.description: EDGE Markets is announcing two new products to solve payment issues in prediction markets, the company shared exclusively with CNBC.

item.title: This popular car-buying rule isn't realistic for most Americans—here's the income needed to make it work
item.description: Even used-car buyers may need a six-figure income to follow this popular car-buying rule.
